task_completion.description: Verifies that the OpenBCI GUI is running and a data stream is initially active.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenBCI GUI window is open. Look for actively moving time-series graphs or a 'Stop Data Stream' button (which indicates that streaming is currently running).

task_completion.description: Verifies that the Focus Widget has been added to the dashboard and is visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A panel or widget window within the OpenBCI GUI dashboard is clearly labeled 'Focus'.

task_completion.description: Verifies that the Concentration metric (which replaces the requested Attention metric) is displayed and active.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Inside the Focus widget, look for a solid color-changing status circle, a time-series line graph, and text indicating a concentration state (e.g., 'Concentrating' or 'Not Concentrating').

task_completion.description: Verifies that the Relaxation metric is displayed and active.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Inside the Focus widget, look for text indicating a relaxation state (e.g., 'Relaxing' or 'Not Relaxing') or 'Relaxation' selected in the widget's dropdown menu.

task_completion.description: Verifies that the data stream was not stopped while the Focus widget is active.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Stop Data Stream' button remains visible (indicating the stream is still active) at the same time the Focus widget is displayed and functioning on the dashboard.

integrity.description: Verifies the agent interacted with the actual OpenBCI GUI application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the graphical user interface of OpenBCI GUI, not a terminal, script, or text editor.

integrity.description: Verifies the agent didn't just print the required text to a terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: The words 'Concentrating', 'Relaxing', and the widget graphics appear inside the graphical Focus widget panel, not as text output in a command-line interface.

integrity.description: Verifies the widget is genuinely rendered by the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Focus widget matches the visual style of the OpenBCI GUI (e.g., dark theme, specific fonts, standard widget borders) and contains the expected solid circle and line graph, rather than being a pasted image or a different application.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target application is 'OpenBCI_GUI'. The requested widget is named 'Focus'. Note that the Focus widget does NOT contain a 'circular gauge' or an 'Attention' metric. Instead, it features a solid, color-changing 'status circle', a time-series line graph, and dropdown metrics for 'Concentration' and 'Relaxation'. The on-screen text will indicate states such as 'Concentrating', 'Not Concentrating', 'Relaxing', or 'Not Relaxing'.
